etymology.description: 网(网) (wǎng, "net") + 維(維) (wéi, "bird tied with string") = net for catching birds -> 羅(羅) (luó, "net")
etymology.interpretation: The character 羅(羅) (luó, "net") is an ideogrammic compound that combines the idea of a net with the concept of a bird tied with a string, visually conveying the purpose of catching birds. This compound reflects its historical usage as a tool in bird catching.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 羅 was simplified to 罗 by removing the silk radical 糸(糸).

examples.chinese: 渔夫撒下了大网(漁夫撒下了大網)，希望能捕到很多鱼(希望能捕到很多魚)。
examples.pinyin: Yúfū sā xiàle dà wǎng, xīwàng néng bǔ dào hěn duō yú.
examples.english: The fisherman cast a big net, hoping to catch many fish.

examples.chinese: 他用罗子(他用羅子)将柳絮筛干净。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng luózi jiāng liǔxù shāi gānjìng.
examples.english: He used a sieve to clean off the willow catkins.

examples.chinese: 迷人的夜晚如同一张魔法网(迷人的夜晚如同一張魔法網)，将人们笼罩其中(將人們籠罩其中)。
examples.pinyin: Mírén de yèwǎn rútóng yī zhāng mófǎ wǎng, jiāng rénmen lǒngzhào qízhōng.
examples.english: The enchanting night is like a magical net, enveloping everyone within it.
